title: The Lion Beaten By The Man.
content:
[1]

     A picture once was shown,
     In which one man, alone,
     Upon the ground had thrown
     A lion fully grown.
     Much gloried at the sight the rabble.
     A lion thus rebuked their babble: - 
     'That you have got the victory there,
     There is no contradiction.
     But, gentles, possibly you are
     The dupes of easy fiction:
     Had we the art of making pictures,
     Perhaps our champion had beat yours!'
meaning:
This evocative piece by Jean de La Fontaine, titled "The Lion Beaten By The Man.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
